title: To Brunichild, Queen of the Franks.

source.title: NPNF-213. Gregory the Great (II), Ephraim Syrus, Aphrahat

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: What good gifts have been conferred on you from above, and with what piety heavenly grace has filled you, this, among all the other proofs of your merits, intimates evidently to all that you both govern the savage hearts of barbarians with the skill of prudent counsel, and (what is still more to your praise), adorn your royal power with wisdom.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: And since, as you are above many nations in both these respects, so also you excel them in the purity of your faith, we have great confidence in your amending what is unlawful.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: For the contents of the letters you have already sent us are witness how your Excellency has embraced our exhortation, and with what devotion you long to fulfil the same.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: But, since He Who is the giver of good dispositions is wont to be their helper also, we trust that He may direct your causes in His loving-kindness all the more favourably as He sees you to be assiduous in His cause.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Do you God’s work, and God will do yours.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Wherefore order a synod to be assembled, and, among other things, as we have before written, studiously prohibit by the definition of a council the sin of simoniacal heresy in your kingdom.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Offer a sacrifice to God by conquering the enemy that is within, that by His help you may conquer the enemies that are without; and that, according to the zeal you evince against His foes, such you may feel Him to be in aiding you.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Believe me, moreover, that, as we have learnt from the experience of many, whatever is gathered together with sin is spent with loss.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: If, then, you wish to lose nothing unjustly, endeavour to the utmost to have nothing got by injustice.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: For in earthly matters loss has always its origin in sin.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: You, therefore, if you wish to stand above adverse nations, if you would speedily, with God’s leave, be victorious over them, receive with trembling the commandments of the same Almighty God, that He Himself may fight for you against your adversaries, Who has promised in Holy Writ, saying, The Lord shall fight for you, and ye shall hold your peace (Exod. xiv.
